purpose: Refresh the settings-manager.ts port ROI verdict against the live 2026-04-27 tree. The earlier 2026-04-26 witness assumed zero non-TS consumers; that premise is now false because hive/core/settings/{schema,store,manager}.hexa exists as an explicit fresh hexa port of packages/coding-agent/src/core/settings-manager.ts, with parity tests and migration notes. This cycle judges realized ROI and residual gaps, not hypothetical demand.
trigger: user 2026-04-27 prompt: kick(ω-cycle) topic='settings-manager.ts port ROI', stratum=drill, axes=design-strategy + falsifier-coverage, witness landing path declared in prompt frontmatter.

noise_envelopes.payload_summary: bridge_pick=bridge_horizons invocation=tool/horizons_bridge.hexa (real fetch deferred to sub-agent or OFFLINE-FALLBACK)

load_bearing_mechanisms: deepMergeSettings with nested object spread and primitive/array override
modifiedFields / modifiedNestedFields / project variants for partial-write merge
environment_overlays: getClearOnShrink() consults HIVE_CLEAR_ON_SHRINK when setting absent
getShowHardwareCursor() consults HIVE_HARDWARE_CURSOR when setting absent
getLanguage() lets HIVE_LANGUAGE override persisted value

documented_divergences: manager.hexa drops TS writeQueue because single-process daemon / IPC single-writer model is assumed
modifiedFields tracking is not publicly exposed; hexa P1 uses full-rewrite semantics
wire compatibility and migration parity are explicitly claimed in README and test_parity.hexa

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: port-still-hypothetical
pre_registered_falsifiers.criterion: The live tree must contain no real hexa settings port; if so, ROI remains speculative.
pre_registered_falsifiers.verdict_check: Fail the positive-ROI thesis if hive/core/settings/{schema,store,manager}.hexa and their tests are absent.

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: non-ts-demand-still-zero
pre_registered_falsifiers.criterion: There must be at least one non-TS surface that now depends on the port, or the old zero-demand objection still stands.
pre_registered_falsifiers.verdict_check: Search for direct settings hexa modules, docs, and reuse of settings-port patterns elsewhere in hive/core.

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: parity-claim-unfounded
pre_registered_falsifiers.criterion: The port must carry concrete parity evidence rather than comments alone.
pre_registered_falsifiers.verdict_check: Require committed parity and manager/store witness files with bidirectional checks against TS semantics.

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: concurrency-or-write-safety-regressed
pre_registered_falsifiers.criterion: Replacing TS proper-lockfile + writeQueue must not leave the hexa path without a defensible write-safety story.
pre_registered_falsifiers.verdict_check: Require a concrete lockfile or single-writer discipline and atomic-write path in-tree.

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: feature-gap-too-wide
pre_registered_falsifiers.criterion: If the hexa port omits load-bearing TS behavior beyond a bounded, documented subset, ROI is negative.
pre_registered_falsifiers.verdict_check: Compare TS reference behavior with README/manager.hexa known divergences and classify omitted semantics.

pre_registered_falsifiers.name: verification-blocker-hidden
pre_registered_falsifiers.criterion: If live execution of the parity witnesses is blocked, the obstruction must be explicit and bounded.
pre_registered_falsifiers.verdict_check: Attempt local execution; if blocked, record exact guardrail and why it does not invalidate static parity evidence.

axes_implemented.decision: The port is realized, not hypothetical. `hive/core/settings/README.md` declares a fresh hexa port, and `schema.hexa`, `store.hexa`, and `manager.hexa` mirror the TS module family explicitly. This alone falsifies the older witness premise that no non-TS realization existed.

Demand is now internalized into the repo. The hexa settings stack is a first-class non-TS consumer of the TS design, and its patterns are already reused elsewhere: `core/auth-storage/*` cites the settings manager/store pattern, `core/model-registry/registry.hexa` copies `_safe_parse_json`, and `core/IMPORTS.md` now lists `settings-manager` under existing hexa-lang code. The old F1-style objection of zero non-TS demand no longer survives contact with the tree.

falsifier_verdicts.F101: PASS — non-TS demand is realized by the hexa port itself and adjacent reuse

axes_implemented.decision: `test_parity.hexa` is not a hand-wave: it encodes four cross-impl checks (hexa write -> TS read, TS write -> hexa read, deep_merge parity, migrate_settings parity) using a temporary Node probe that re-expresses the TS reference semantics. `test_manager.hexa` and `test_store.hexa` cover scoped getters/setters, drain_errors, nested merges, clamping, and persistence. The parity claim is therefore backed by committed executable witnesses, even though local execution was blocked on this host.

falsifier_verdicts.F102: PASS — parity evidence is committed and concrete
falsifier_verdicts.F105: PASS_with_obstruction — execution blocked locally but the blocker is explicit and audited

axes_implemented.decision: The hexa port does not simply drop TS write safety. `store.hexa` provides atomic `.tmp + mv` writes plus `write_settings_locked`, and `core/util/lockfile.hexa` implements advisory locks with stale detection via pid + acquire time TTL. `manager.hexa` documents a different concurrency model: no async `writeQueue`, single-process daemon or IPC single writer, and full-rewrite semantics. This is a change in strategy, but it is a documented one with in-tree primitives, not an unguarded regression.

falsifier_verdicts.F103: PASS — replacement safety model exists and is explicit

axes_implemented.decision: The port is not full semantic parity. The TS reference layers in env overrides (`HIVE_CLEAR_ON_SHRINK`, `HIVE_HARDWARE_CURSOR`, `HIVE_LANGUAGE`) and retains partial-write semantics through modified-field tracking plus writeQueue serialization. The hexa port intentionally omits public modified-field tracking and chooses full-rewrite semantics; its README marks this as P1 scope. These are bounded divergences because they are documented, narrow, and do not erase the main value delivered by the port: non-TS read/write/merge/migration capability over `settings.json` with wire compatibility.

falsifier_verdicts.F104: PASS_with_gaps — divergences are bounded and documented, not unbounded parity failure

axis_orthogonality_check.method: lens-disjointness on realization, demand, parity evidence, write-safety strategy, and residual-gap bounding
axis_orthogonality_check.verdict: PASS — K=5 axes remain below the raw-48 correlation threshold

tier_1_promotions.proposition: The settings-manager.ts port has positive realized ROI. The repo now owns a reusable non-TS settings subsystem in hexa (`core/settings/*`) with committed parity witnesses, atomic write primitives, migration compatibility, and downstream pattern reuse across adjacent hexa modules.
tier_1_promotions.falsifier_pass_pattern: F100=PASS, F101=PASS, F102=PASS, F103=PASS, F104=PASS_with_gaps, F105=PASS_with_obstruction
tier_1_promotions.promotion_gate: Satisfied — every pre-registered falsifier is retired or bounded; none forces a negative-ROI verdict on the live tree.

tier_1_promotions.proposition: Keep `packages/coding-agent/src/core/settings-manager.ts` as the semantic reference while the hexa port remains P1. The next parity-tightening steps, if needed, are selective: decide whether env-overlay behavior should migrate into hexa getters, and whether full-rewrite semantics need a narrower partial-write path under multi-writer pressure.
tier_1_promotions.promotion_gate: Satisfied — current gaps are bounded enough for coexistence, not for deleting the TS reference.

roi_verdict.summary: The earlier negative-ROI thesis depended on zero non-TS demand. That premise is obsolete: a live hexa port now exists, brings concrete utility, and ships committed parity witnesses plus a write-safety model. The port is not perfect parity, but the remaining deltas are narrow and explicitly documented. Net result: positive ROI has already been captured.
residual_risks: hexa manager does not mirror TS env-overlay getters for clearOnShrink, showHardwareCursor, and language
hexa manager chooses full-rewrite semantics rather than TS modified-field partial writes
local witness execution on this macOS host is blocked by the hexa darwin landing guard unless the environment explicitly opts out

F100_port_still_hypothetical.evidence: hive/core/settings/{schema,store,manager,README,test_store,test_manager,test_parity}.hexa exists and self-describes as a fresh port

F101_non_ts_demand_still_zero.evidence: the hexa settings subsystem is itself a realized non-TS consumer; auth-storage and model-registry explicitly reuse settings-port patterns

F102_parity_claim_unfounded.evidence: test_parity.hexa encodes four bidirectional TS/hexa checks; test_manager.hexa and test_store.hexa cover manager/store behavior

F103_concurrency_or_write_safety_regress.evidence: store.hexa implements atomic writes and locked writes; util/lockfile.hexa provides stale-detecting advisory locks; README documents single-writer model

F104_feature_gap_too_wide.evidence: gaps are limited to documented P1 omissions, chiefly env overlays and partial-write/writeQueue semantics

F105_verification_blocker_hidden.evidence: direct test execution returned `hexa_interp shim: hexa-exec container unavailable on darwin host — refusing Mac-bare exec (rc=76). Override: HEXA_SHIM_NO_DARWIN_LANDING=1`; blocker is explicit and bounded

obstructions_documented.detail: Attempting to run `hexa hive/core/settings/test_{parity,manager,store}.hexa` on 2026-04-27 returned `hexa_interp shim: hexa-exec container unavailable on darwin host — refusing Mac-bare exec (rc=76). Override: HEXA_SHIM_NO_DARWIN_LANDING=1`. Per honesty constraints the cycle did not override the guardrail silently.
